_meta.licensing: Each entry's per-source license is in its `license` field and governs the archived raw bytes plus any verbatim source text quoted in `normalized_content` (e.g. `narrative_excerpt`, `declaration_text`). Those third-party excerpts remain under the source's own license (WHO CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO; Imperial restricted-use assumption pending primary license footer; Wikipedia CC BY-SA 4.0; US CDC public domain). The CC BY 4.0 license declared for this repo in LICENSES.md covers only the schema, structure, SHA-256 hashes, and my own annotations in this file, not quoted third-party text or extracted third-party tables. Confirm permissions before commercial reuse or redistribution of restricted publisher material.
_meta.scope: Live provenance registry for public sources for the BDBV 2026 outbreak. Entries with raw_archive_status=public_bytes ship one fetched-and-archived byte payload under data/bundibugyo-2026/raw/<sha256>. Entries with raw_archive_status=private_restricted_bytes retain URL, timestamp, hash, and extracted factual metadata but do not redistribute publisher bytes.
_meta.verification_workflow: For public_bytes entries, re-run shasum -a 256 against the raw file to verify. For private_restricted_bytes entries, the content_hash records the original retrieval hash but the raw payload is intentionally not redistributed.

care_continuity: CTE, CME, and creche construction monitoring continued at CME, ISTM Nyankunde, HGR Rwampara, and Elykia.
Nutrition follow-up continued in all CTEs.
Five health facilities were evaluated in Nizi and recovery plans were prepared for evaluated facilities.
community_engagement: Nord-Kivu sensitized 11,853 people, engaged 77 community actors, used 18 radio channels for shows/spots, and logged 11 community alerts.
Sud-Kivu advocacy involved 45 leaders in Katana, a CREC meeting in Miti-Murhesa, and briefing of 10 CREC members.
Ituri evaluated Mongbwalu activities and engaged Shari authorities around funeral-prayer risk near the Balidja morgue in Rwampara.
context: The affected Ituri area borders Uganda and South Sudan and has chronic humanitarian constraints, cross-border movement, artisanal mining, armed-group activity, and more than one million internally displaced people.
The outbreak origin remains described around Mongbwalu in Djugu territory, about 70 km from Bunia.
The outbreak was officially declared on 2026-05-15.
coordination: Evaluation and coordination missions continued in Beni and Butembo.
CHU Cinquantenaire Kinshasa agent briefing was underway.
A SGI, partner, and national-expert session in Rwampara focused on surveillance, contact listing, and follow-up.
National and provincial SGI training closed during the reporting cycle.
highlights: 17 new confirmed cases including 5 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-11.
New confirmed cases were in Bunia (6), Rwampara (5), Mongbwalu (4), and Nizi (2).
COUSP and national incident-management teams supervised CTE Butembo and Katwa.
INSP leadership and a COUSP/WHO multidisciplinary team arrived in Butembo.
CTE Rwampara received a cardiac monitor and pulse oximeter.
infection_prevention_control: Ituri continued training and briefings in Komanda, Nyankunde, and Bambu, and decontaminated two health facilities and two households.

Nord-Kivu evaluated four health facilities, decontaminated two, briefed 46 of 56 providers across 14 of 17 facilities, evaluated exposure risk for 15 of 36 providers, and supported 26 facilities in Goma, Karisimbi, and Nyiragongo.
Sud-Kivu decontaminated 12 households and 9 toilets, supported safe sampling work, briefed hygienists at CS Kahungu, and supported Kahungu and Kalagane.
laboratory: Ituri reported 35 samples collected and analyzed, with 17 positives and 48.6% positivity.
Nord-Kivu reported 172 samples pending with a delay greater than five days.
logistics_security: Medicines and IPC supplies were delivered to Rwampara, and CTE/CME Bunia materials were delivered.
Bunia and Rwampara were relatively calm, while armed-group activity in Djugu, Irumu, and Mambasa continued to limit humanitarian access.
psea: Ituri PSEA sessions included PAM-supported participants, FHI360 participants, and a leader session with code-of-conduct signatures recorded.
Nord-Kivu second-wave sensitization in Goma and Beni reached community relays and leaders, with code-of-conduct signatures recorded.
Sud-Kivu briefed response actors and providers at CS Kabushwa and teachers in Miti-Murhesa and Lwiro.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 28 reports data through 2026-06-11 and was published 2026-06-12.
The outbreak remains reported across 3 provinces and 29 health zones; no newly touched health zone is reported for this cycle.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 646 of 689 DRC confirmed cases (93.8%).
Nord-Kivu is harmonized to 40 confirmed cases from the prior 44-count state; the SitRep footnote attributes this to national SGI data harmonization.
Sud-Kivu has reported no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26.
redactions: Page 11 contact details are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 419 alerts were reported and 377 were investigated, producing a 90.0% investigation rate.
168 suspected cases of the day were validated, including 64 deaths.
234 new contacts were listed in Lita (115), Nizi (101), Bambu (14), and Kambala (4).
Rwampara and Bunia contact follow-up data were unavailable despite representing 55.6% of Ituri contacts, lowering the global follow-up rate to 28.4%.

challenges.finding: Reluctance to post-mortem swabbing can delay confirmation and underestimate mortality.
challenges.response: Strengthen community engagement and religious-leader involvement.

challenges.finding: Normalized CTE capacity remains insufficient despite Rwampara opening.
challenges.response: Accelerate planned CTE construction.

challenges.finding: MEG supplies remain insufficient in targeted HGRs.
challenges.response: Continue advocacy and resource mobilization.

challenges.finding: IPC materials are insufficient in Nord-Kivu, increasing nosocomial risk.
challenges.response: Urgent supply reinforcement.

challenges.finding: Alert reporting remains weak across the three affected provinces.
challenges.response: Strengthen community surveillance.

challenges.finding: Insufficient resources across pillars slow operations; the SitRep cites a USD 21.5M gap.
challenges.response: Resource mobilization.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Rwampara and Bunia contact follow-up data not available despite 55.6% of Ituri contacts.

priorities.priority: Train PCR RADIONE providers
Brief affected/high-risk zones on MVE/SBC surveillance
Brief community agents in Ituri on MVE/SBC
Support INSP leadership supervision across technical pillars
Catch up Ituri contact follow-up
Reinforce IPC and staff training in Nyankunde and other nosocomial zones
Deploy daily standardized KPI dashboard
Catch up Nord-Kivu pending samples
Execute Nord-Kivu actions: Goma recovered exit, security incident response, Virunga HGR IPC training, and household CREC expansion
Support Sud-Kivu with two vehicles, Miti-Murhesa internet kit, and IT/ECZS communication credits

SitRep28 reports no newly touched health zone at the end of 2026-06-11; affected footprint remains 29/104 health zones.
Nord-Kivu was harmonized downward from the SitRep27 44-confirmed state to 40 confirmed; treat this as a source revision, not a negative incident delta.
Daily suspected cases (168) and suspected deaths (64) are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative suspected deaths.

care_continuity: Five patients were declared recovered after negative control tests.
Creche and CTE construction monitoring continued at CME, ISTM Nyankunde, HGR Nyankunde, HGR Rwampara, and Elykia.
Across all CTEs, 470 hot meals were provided to patients and accompanying groups.
Finalization of CTE/CME-Bunia work continued, with eight tents expected to be operational by Monday 2026-06-15.
community_engagement: Nord-Kivu sensitized students, households, public-place audiences, leaders, and transport actors and reported 503 reluctance/refusal/resistance events resolved.
Sud-Kivu used radio, household visits, leader engagement, and school sensitization across Miti-Murhesa and Katana.
Ituri oriented leaders for incident prevention, conducted mass sensitization and household visits, trained relays in Mongbwalu, and supported surveillance and IPC around Rwampara areas.
The SitRep notes recurring community feedback that EDS teams sometimes arrive understaffed, prompting unprotected community assistance at burial sites.
context: The affected Ituri area borders Uganda and South Sudan and has chronic humanitarian constraints, cross-border movement, artisanal mining, armed-group activity, and more than one million internally displaced people.
The outbreak origin remains described around Mongbwalu in Djugu territory, about 70 km from Bunia.
The outbreak was officially declared on 2026-05-15.
coordination: The RDC-Ouganda cross-border meeting on the MVE-17 situation continued on 2026-06-12.
Training of MCZ and AC for the Aru pool continued.
highlights: 72 new confirmed cases including 29 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-13 in Ituri (56) and Nord-Kivu (16).
New confirmed cases were in Mongbwalu (25), Bunia (13), Rwampara (6), Nyankunde (5), Komanda (3), Nizi (2), Damas (1), Nia-Nia (1), Butembo (11), Katwa (4), and Mabalako (1).
Two new affected health zones were reported: Nia-Nia in Ituri and Mabalako in Nord-Kivu.
Five patients were declared recovered: Rwampara (3), Mongbwalu (1), and Mambasa (1).
infection_prevention_control: Ituri opened rings around five confirmed cases, including three in Bunia and two in Rwampara, while noting an IPC-kit gap.
Ituri reported EDS completion activity in Mongbwalu, Bunia, Komanda, Rwampara, and Bambu, plus decontamination in health facilities, households, and public buildings.
Nord-Kivu evaluated seven facilities in Beni, supported 59 facilities across Goma, Beni, Butembo, and Katwa, briefed 61 providers, and continued IPC training with WHO technical support.
Sud-Kivu received PPE/IPC supplies, assessed IPC needs at Nyakadaka, and briefed 65 providers on standard precautions and decontamination.
laboratory: Ituri reported 139 samples collected and analyzed, with 56 positives and 40.3% positivity.
Sud-Kivu reported 8 samples collected and analyzed, all negative.
Nord-Kivu reported 42 samples collected, 34 analyzed for Bundibugyo strain, and 16 positive results, leaving 8 samples pending from the day's batch.
logistics_security: The response received 164 mattresses and 140 beds for health facilities.
Twenty-eight vehicles and two ambulances were deployed across response pillars.
Motorcycles were delivered to Logo and Aungba health zones.
The security situation in and around Bunia remained calm but unpredictable.
psea: Community sensitization on complaint mechanisms continued.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 30 reports data through 2026-06-13 and was published 2026-06-14; public INSP evidence did not expose SitRep 29 before this release.

Nord-Kivu rose to 62 confirmed cases and carries the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 54.8%.

redactions: Page 11 contact details are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 507 alerts were reported and 460 investigated, producing a 90.7% investigation rate.
136 suspected cases of the day were validated, including 49 deaths.
6,275 contacts were under follow-up and 3,548 were seen, producing 56.5% follow-up coverage across the three provinces.
449 new contacts were listed in eight Ituri health zones, and 105 Sud-Kivu contacts completed 21 days of follow-up.

The challenge table cites 96 health-worker infections with 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%), a Nord-Kivu CTE saturation of 138.8%, a Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog amid a 54.2% provincial CFR, an isolation-structure gap of about 20 centers, and a financing gap of about USD 20 million.

A community-death sub-analysis in SitRep51 (94.2% of analysis-cohort deaths occurring in the community, concentrated in Mongbwalu, Bunia and Katwa; late presentation to care) is internally inconsistent with the cumulative confirmed-death total and is treated as qualitative context only, never as a denominator.

External response milestones reported outside the INSP SitRep are tracked as separate context in the LOVS narrative/synthesis, not in the INSP denominators: the WHO Emergency Use Listing of the first BDBV molecular RT-PCR diagnostic (2026-07-02) supports the SitRep-noted decentralization of diagnostic capacity; and the response financing picture (INSP-cited ~USD 20 million pillar-implementation gap; separately, an Africa CDC response requirement of ~USD 1.4 billion with pledges only ~13% released, a White House/OMB supplemental request of ~USD 1.4 billion still before Congress, and OCHA/UNDP macro-humanitarian context) is recorded as distinct facets and not collapsed into a single figure. The clinical trial that began at CTE CME Rwampara on 2026-07-04 is an INSP-reported operational milestone, kept as narrative context and not a denominator.
